title: Equilibrium spin currents in altermagnet junctions: Josephson-like and anomalous transport
abstract: Altermagnets (AMs) offer a compelling platform for exploring novel spin-dependent phenomena in materials with zero net macroscopic magnetization. In this work, we theoretically investigate the emergence of equilibrium spin currents (ESCs) in two-dimensional AM heterostructures using a tight-binding lattice model. We first study an AM-normal metal-AM (AM-NM-AM) junction and demonstrate that the $σ_y$-polarized ESC exhibits a characteristic Josephson-like behavior, fundamentally governed by the relative angle ($θ$) between the Néel vectors of the two AMs pointing in $xz$-plane. Crucially, we show that replacing the central normal metal with a $p$-wave magnet (PM) induces an anomalous ESC. Analogous to the anomalous Josephson effect, the breaking of spatial inversion symmetry by the PM allows a finite, dissipationless spin current to flow even when the Néel vectors are perfectly aligned ($θ=0$). We establish that this anomalous transport is driven by an asymmetry in the quantum phases accumulated by right- and left-moving electrons undergoing spin-flip reflections. Finally, we show that the critical ESC exhibits pronounced fluctuations as a function of band filling, which we attribute to mesoscopic quantum size effects, including transverse subband quantization and longitudinal Fabry-Pérot resonances. Our findings highlight the potential of altermagnet junctions for designing dissipationless, phase-tunable spintronic devices.
summary: This theoretical work explores equilibrium spin currents in altermagnet junctions using a tight-binding model. It distinguishes between conventional, angle-dependent Josephson-like transport across normal metals and an anomalous current induced by p-wave magnets. The findings highlight that symmetry breaking mechanisms can enable dissipationless spin flow even when magnetic alignment is perfect.
main_problem: The study investigates the emergence of equilibrium spin currents (ESCs) in altermagnet junctions, contrasting conventional Josephson-like transport with anomalous spin current flow.
main_result: Anomalous ESCs can be induced by replacing a normal metal spacer with a p-wave magnet, allowing finite spin current even when the magnetic order parameters are perfectly aligned. The critical current also exhibits mesoscopic quantum size effect fluctuations.
method: The investigation uses theoretical calculations based on a tight-binding lattice model to diagonalize the full Hamiltonian and calculate the expectation value of the spin current operator over occupied states.
model_or_system: The system models two-dimensional Altermagnet (AM) heterostructures connected by a central region, which can be either a Normal Metal (NM) or a p-wave Magnet (PM). The physics is governed by spin-dependent hopping terms and the relative angle ($	heta$) between the AM Néel vectors.
key_observables: Equilibrium Spin Current (ESC), specifically $\sigma_y$-polarized, and its dependence on the relative angle ($	heta$), PM angle ($\phi$), and band filling ($f$).
important_parameters: Relative angle ($	heta$) between AM Néel vectors; p-wave magnet angle ($\phi$); electron filling fraction ($f$).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes spin remains a good quantum number in the central scattering region, and it models the system using a tight-binding lattice approach.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the dependence of ESC on $	heta$ for both NM (Josephson-like) and PM (anomalous) spacers. Other figures show the dependence on the PM angle ($\phi$) and pronounced fluctuations versus band filling ($f$).
paper_structure: The paper systematically analyzes the spin current in AM-NM-AM junctions to establish Josephson-like behavior, then extends the model to AM-PM-AM junctions to demonstrate the anomalous transport mechanism driven by symmetry breaking. Finally, it discusses quantum size effects and symmetries.
why_interesting: While focused on condensed matter magnetism, the study of dissipationless spin currents and phase-tunable transport mechanisms in novel magnetic materials like altermagnets has strong parallels with engineered superconducting junctions studied in AMO physics (e.g., Josephson effect).
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic diagram of the magnetic heterostructure. The setup consists of a central region (either an NM or a PM) sandwiched between two AMs with a relative N´eel vector angle θ. This phase-like parameter θ governs the flow of an equilibrium spin current through the junction, establishing a spintronic analog to the conventional Josephson effect. The two AMs have their N´eel vectors pointing in xz-plane, whereas the N´eel vector of the central PM points along y-direction.

FIG. 3. ESC as a function of the relative angle θ between the N´eel vectors of the two AMs in an AM-PM-AM junction. Note the presence of a non-zero anomalous ESC, Js,a y , at θ = 0. The legend indicates the values of (ϕ, Js,c y , Js,a y /Js,c y ) for each respective curve. Note that anomalous ESC vanishes for ϕ = π/2 due to restored inversion symmetry along x- direction. System parameters are set to: La = 20, Lp = 10, Ly = 10, ta = 0.5t, tl = 0.2t, tr = 0.1t, tp = 0.5t, and f = 0.5.

FIG. 2. Equilibrium spin current (ESC) as a function of the relative angle between the N´eel vectors of the two AMs in an AM-NM-AM junction. The various curves correspond to different filling factors f, as indicated in the legend. System parameters are set to: La = 20, Lp = 10, Ly = 10, ta = 0.5, tl = 0.2t, tr = 0.1t, and tp = 0.

FIG. 4. (a) Anomalous ESC versus ϕ the angle made by the crystallographic axis along which the band bottoms for the two spin sectors are separated in PM with x-direction (b) Crit- ical current versus ϕ. Parameters are same as in Fig. 3, except f = 0.25.
FIG. 5. Critical ESC versus filling factor. Parameters are same as in Fig. 2.
